sections.heading: RT
sections.content:
RT is a treatment option for patients with unresectable HCC, or for those who are medically inoperable due to comorbidity. It can be used for locoregional control or palliation [HCC-6, HCC-H]. SBRT can be considered as an alternative to ablation/embolization or when these therapies have failed or are contraindicated [HCC-H].

"principles": ["Image-guided RT (IGRT) is strongly recommended when using RT, IMRT, and SBRT to improve accuracy and reduce toxicity [HCC-H].", "Tumors may be amenable to RT (3D-CRT, IMRT, or SBRT) [HCC-H].", "The majority of safety and efficacy data are from patients with CTP A liver disease, with growing experience in CTP B7; safety for CTP B8 or CTP C is not established [HCC-H].", "Proton beam therapy may be appropriate in specific situations due to reduced dose to normal tissues [HCC-H].", "Palliative RT is appropriate for symptom control from metastatic lesions (bone, brain) or extensive liver tumor burden in patients with CTP A or B cirrhosis [HCC-H]."],

"dose_frameworks": [{"name": "Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy (SBRT)", "total_dose": "27.5–60 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "Delivered in 3–5 fractions", "fractions": "3–5", "schedule": "Hypofractionated", "indication": "Preferred for patients with 1 to 5 tumors, assuming sufficient uninvolved liver and liver tolerance. Considered as alternative to ablation/embolization or after failure/contraindication of those therapies [HCC-H]."}, {"name": "Hypofractionation", "total_dose": "37.5–72 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "N/A", "fractions": "10–15", "schedule": "Hypofractionated", "indication": "Acceptable option for intrahepatic tumors [HCC-H]."}, {"name": "Conventional Fractionation", "total_dose": "50–66 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "N/A", "fractions": "25–33", "schedule": "Conventional", "indication": "Historical or specific clinical scenarios not detailed further [HCC-H]."}, {"name": "Palliative Radiation Therapy", "total_dose": "8 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "8 Gy", "fractions": "1", "schedule": "Single fraction", "indication": "For palliation of pain from extensive burden of cancer in the liver [HCC-H]."}],

"approaches": [{"name": "SBRT", "dose_fractionation": "Typically 3–5 fractions, total dose 27.5–60 Gy (BED10 >72 Gy preferred if constraints met) [HCC-H].", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Not routinely specified for HCC SBRT in this guideline.", "indication": "Patients with unresectable HCC, not candidates for ablation/embolization or after failure/contraindication. Can be used for 1-5 tumors with sufficient liver reserve [HCC-H].", "key_trial": "Randomized evidence supports its usefulness (e.g., NRG/RTOG 1112 phase 3 trial) [HCC-H].", "toxicities": "Risk of radiation-induced liver disease (RILD), especially with compromised liver function. Requires strict adherence to dose constraints [HCC-H]."}, {"name": "Proton Beam Therapy", "dose_fractionation": "Hypofractionated; specific regimen not detailed but cited in references.", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Not specified.", "indication": "May be appropriate in specific situations to reduce dose to adjacent normal tissues [HCC-H].", "key_trial": "Randomized clinical trials comparing to TACE or RFA cited [HCC-H].", "toxicities": "Associated with reduction of dose to adjacent normal tissues compared to photon therapy [HCC-H]."}, {"name": "Palliative RT", "dose_fractionation": "8 Gy in 1 fraction for liver pain; other schedules for bone/brain metastases not detailed.", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Not specified.", "indication": "Symptom control and/or prevention of complications from metastatic HCC lesions (bone, brain) or extensive liver tumor burden [HCC-H].", "key_trial": "CCTG HE1 trial cited for palliative RT vs. best supportive care [HCC-H].", "toxicities": "Depends on site treated; generally low for single-fraction liver palliation."}]

timeline.title: Regimen
timeline.body: SBRT: 27.5–60 Gy in 3–5 fractions; Hypofractionation: 37.5–72 Gy in 10–15 fractions; Conventional: 50–66 Gy in 25–33 fractions; Palliative: 8 Gy single fraction
